strengths: Nearly 20 years of continuous software engineering across web, mobile, desktop, embedded, and low-latency distributed systems — comfortably exceeding the 15-year bar with unusual technical breadth.

Current hands-on AI and cloud work at re:cinq: building AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and frameworks for agentic workflows, alongside prior AWS CDK and Kubernetes experience — directly matching the builder/code fluency the role prefers.

Proven entrepreneurial and thought-leadership track record: founder of GISCollective (an open-source geo-data platform with real-world adoption via Green Map) plus conference talks (FOSS4G) and multi-day workshops, evidencing the 'inspire and advise others' responsibility.
concerns: No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field; formal education is a Bachelor's in Computer Science.
Job titles have been Senior/Staff level rather than Principal or Director-level IC, so demonstrated scope at the strategic-advisory altitude the role demands is not explicit on the resume.
Dedicated AI/ML work is recent (2025 onward); 'recognized expertise in AI with demonstrated AI products' is emerging rather than established.

key_factors.factor: Two decades of full-stack, systems, and infrastructure engineering breadth
Active builder of AI-agent infrastructure and cloud-native tooling (MCP, agentic frameworks, AWS, Kubernetes)
Founder experience plus public teaching and speaking record

response.justification: This candidate presents a compelling case for a Principal Engineer role centered on AI strategy: he clears the 15-year experience requirement with room to spare, and that experience is exceptionally broad — from embedded systems and microservices handling millions of requests per second to full-stack product work — giving him the cross-domain fluency a strategy-focused IC needs. He is not an AI theorist but an active AI builder, currently shipping agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, which maps directly onto the role's emphasis on demonstrated AI-powered development and cloud expertise. His founder experience with GISCollective satisfies the entrepreneurial preference, and his sustained record of workshops, courses, and conference talks evidences exactly the kind of insight-sharing and inspiration the responsibilities call for. While his formal titles trail the Director-level framing and his AI specialization is recent, the trajectory, breadth, and builder credibility make him a strong, defensible candidate to advance.
